name: Omb Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering high-yield CDs (up to 4.25% APY), checking/savings accounts, home loans, business loans, and treasury services with both digital and branch-based service.
description_long: OMB Bank is a full-service community bank that combines digital banking convenience with relationship-driven local service. The bank emphasizes accessibility (AudioEye enabled website) and serves customers across multiple financial life stages. The bank positions itself as a values-driven institution focused on personalized guidance rather than transactional-only banking. The bank's primary offerings include certificates of deposit with competitive short-, medium-, and long-term rates (3-month to 27-month terms), checking and savings accounts, residential home loans, business loans, agricultural loans, and treasury/cash management services for businesses. Their CD rates are prominently featured with a 5-month CD at 4.25% APY, 7-month at 4.15% APY, and 9-month at 4.05% APY. All deposit products carry FDIC insurance. Minimum CD deposit is $1,000 with no setup or maintenance fees. OMB Bank distinguishes itself through emphasis on personalized service (featuring staff like Rebeccah Hicks who help customers navigate loan decisions) and industry-specific lending expertise in agriculture, business, and residential markets. The bank uses community partnerships (such as collaboration with Missouri State University's Jackie Stiles) and offers tools like account-finder questionnaires to match customers with appropriate products. They highlight local lending expertise and relationship-based decision-making rather than automated processes. As a traditional community bank, OMB Bank serves customers seeking FDIC-insured savings vehicles and personalized lending, but the website provides limited detail on fee structures, minimum account balances for checking/savings, APYs on non-CD products, specific loan terms, or competitive rate comparisons. Customers should verify current rates and terms directly, as CD rates are explicitly marked with asterisks indicating promotional/limited-time offers.
services: Treasury services and cash management for businesses
Online banking and digital account access
Account-finder tool to match products to customer needs
pros: High-yield CD rates competitive in current market (4.25% APY on 5-month CD, 4.15% on 7-month)
All deposit accounts FDIC-insured up to applicable limits
Multiple CD term options (3 to 27 months) allow flexible savings strategies
No setup or maintenance fees on CDs; low $1,000 minimum deposit
Online account opening for CDs available in minutes plus physical branch locations
Specialized lending expertise in agriculture, business, and residential sectors
Personalized customer service approach with named loan officers vs. automated underwriting
Accessible website design with AudioEye toolbar for assistive technology users
cons: Website does not disclose checking/savings account APYs, monthly fees, or minimum balances
CD rates explicitly marked as promotional/limited-time, suggesting rates may decrease
No information on mortgage rates, business loan terms, or fee schedules available online
Limited transparency on loan approval criteria, timelines, or specific qualification requirements
No details on non-deposit services pricing (wire transfers, check cashing, etc.)
best_for: Savers seeking high-yield FDIC-insured CDs with flexible short-to-medium term options
Homebuyers in the bank's service area wanting personalized mortgage guidance and local expertise
Agricultural operations and small businesses seeking specialized lending relationships
Community-minded customers who value relationship banking over purely digital-only service
diagnosis: OMB Bank is best for savers prioritizing FDIC-insured high-yield CDs and customers needing personalized lending, particularly homebuyers and agricultural/business operators in their service area. The primary caveat is that most product details (checking/savings rates, loan terms, fee structures) are absent from the website, requiring direct contact with the bank for accurate rate and cost comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
